rows.source_wording_excerpt: The older specimen, which we refer to hereafter as HXH, was found at the Early Neolithic site of Herxheim and is dated to 5,223-5,040 cal. BCE. The younger specimen, CTC, was found in Cherry Tree Cave and corresponds to the End Neolithic period in Central Europe.
rows.normalization_rule: keep one normalized interval and preserve its evidence class and precision posture
rows.uncertainty_note: Chronology falls back to the current project-level context row.

temporal_semantics.comparison_note: Chronology falls back to the current project-level context row.
temporal_semantics.provenance_excerpt: The older specimen, which we refer to hereafter as HXH, was found at the Early Neolithic site of Herxheim and is dated to 5,223-5,040 cal. BCE. The younger specimen, CTC, was found in Cherry Tree Cave and corresponds to the End Neolithic period in Central Europe.
